mission.title: signoz: [config] require tls for alertmanager (SIGNOZ_ALERTMANAGER_SIGNOZ_GLOBAL_SMTP__REQUIRE__TLS) is not working
mission.description: [config] require tls for alertmanager (SIGNOZ_ALERTMANAGER_SIGNOZ_GLOBAL_SMTP__REQUIRE__TLS) is not working . Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify signoz troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your signoz deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz -n signoz --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review signoz configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant signoz configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz
kubectl get configmap -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=signoz
```
I've been trying to get SMTP forwarding configured to work with alerting on an Ubuntu install of Signoz v0.84.1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [config] require tls for alertmanager…
steps.description:
#### Current Approach
Preserve the explicit field value before merge, restore it after in both of the mentioned instances:
```go
smtpRequireTls := globalConfig.SMTPRequireTLS
err := mergo.Merge(&globalConfig, config.DefaultGlobalConfig())
if err != nil {
    return nil,
```yaml
This minimal patch fixes the problem without altering struct definitions or wider behaviour but is not future proof.
Any future boolean or similar field requires another manual restore line.

<!-- Describe how reviewers can test this PR -->
---

## ✅ Changes
- [x] Bug fix: Fixes boolean merge issue where `SIGNOZ_ALERTMANAGER_SIGNOZ_GLOBAL_SMTP__REQUIRE__TLS=false` was ignored due to Mergo treating `false` as a zero value and overwriting it with the default `true`.


---

steps.title: Confirm [config] require tls for alertmanager… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz -n signoz --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n signoz --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
#### Current Approach
Preserve the explicit field value before merge, restore it after in both of the mentioned instances:
```go
smtpRequireTls := globalConfig.SMTPRequireTLS
err := mergo.Merge(&globalConfig, config.DefaultGlobalConfig())
if err != nil {
    return nil, err
}
globalConfig.SMTPRequireTLS = smtpRequireTls
``` 
This minimal patch fixes the problem without altering struct definitions
codeSnippets:
This minimal patch fixes the problem without altering struct definitions or wider behaviour but is not future proof.
Any future boolean or similar field requires another manual restore line.

<!-- Describe how reviewers can test this PR -->

## ✅ Changes
- [x] Bug fix: Fixes boolean merge issue where `SIGNOZ_ALERTMANAGER_SIGNOZ_GLOBAL_SMTP__REQUIRE__TLS=false` was ignored due to Mergo treating `false` as a zero value and overwriting it with the default `true`.
